LAW AND MOTION 301, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. IT IS NOT CLEAR THAT PLAINTIFF HAD A RIGHT TO CONSENT AS A CONDITION TO DEFENDANT'S RIGHT TO ASSIGN AND EVEN IF PLAINTIFF DID, IT IS LIKELY THAT NEW YORK LAW WOULD GOVERN THE ENFORCEABILITY OF THAT CLAUSE AND WOULD RELEGATE PLAINTIFF TO A CLAIM FOR DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES FAVOR IT GIVEN THE NATURE OF ITS INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY AND ITS FAILURE TO TENDER THE AMOUNT OWED TO EITHER DEFNDANT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-23-2011 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT EUROHYPO AG, NEW YORK BRANCH'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OFF CALENDAR, CASE REMOVED TO FEDERAL COURT. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SQUARE MILE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC'S AND POST INVESTORS LLC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OFF CALENDAR, CASE REMOVED TO FEDERAL COURT. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Order To Show Cause Re Perliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Andrew Zepeda, Esq. and Bruce Lurie (Court Call Appearance) of Lurie, Zepeda, Schmaltz & Hogan, 310-274-8700 and Brendan Macauley, Esq. of Nossaman, 415-398-3600 for plaintiff and moving party Festival Retail Fund
Gregory Dresser, Esq. of Morrison & Foerster, 415-268-6396 for defendant and opposing party Eurohypo AG
Theodore Griffinger, Esq. of Stein & Lubin, 415-981-0550 for defendants and opposing parties Post Investor & Square Mile

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows --
Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A LIKLIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. IT IS NOT CLEAR THAT PLAINTIFF HAD A RIGHT TO CONSENT AS A CONDITION TO DEFENDANT'S RIGHT TO ASSIGN AND EVEN IF PLAINTIFF DID, IT IS LIKELY THAT NEW YORK LAW WOULD GOVERN THE ENFORCEABILITY OF THAT CLAUSE AND WOULD RELEGATE PLAINTIFF TO A CLAIM FOR DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES FAVOR IT GIVEN THE NATURE OF ITS INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY AND ITS FAILURE TO TENDER THE AMOUNT OWED TO EITHER DEFNDANT. PER DIEM FEES WERE ASSESSED FOR ARGUING OVER AN HOUR PER GOVT. CODE AND CRC CODE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Carol Karen CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)
